heading: Art. 12. Prohibition of confinement of members of the armed forces with enemy prisoners and certain others
label: 812 Art. 12. Prohibition of confinement of members of the armed forces with enemy prisoners and certain others
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)81250:566.(Art. 12), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 112The words “of the United States” are omitted as surplusage. The word “may” is substituted for the word “shall”.

Amendments2016— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “No member of the armed forces may be placed in confinement in immediate association with enemy prisoners or other foreign nationals not members of the armed forces.”Pub. L. 114–328

Effective Date of 2016 AmendmentAmendment by effective on , as designated by the President, with implementing regulations and provisions relating to applicability to various situations, see and Ex. Ord. No. 13825, set out as notes under .Pub. L. 114–328section 5542 of Pub. L. 114–328section 801 of this titleJan. 1, 2019

children.text: who are detained under the law of war and are foreign nationals; and

children.label: B
children.text: who are not members of the armed forces.
